payload.text: i want to create a simple promotional site for my app keepy (keepy-app.vercel.app) inspired by the corner app (https://www.corner.inc/?s=tk) I want it modern, aesthetic, and themed bubbles maybe can be floating in certain parts in the background. the page is to explain to users what the app is for, explaining to users how to install the pwa app on ios/android. The story behind the app , the founder of the app, and how to contact me. I want it be interactive and pretty with the brand colors (#c1ff72, #ffe371, #fa8ace, #8ffbff, #ffa55b, #09253c, white) i want the site to be bright and fresh

**Defining the Project Scope**

Okay, I've got a handle on the Keepy landing page goal. I'm focusing on the user's need for a promotional page. I'm taking inspiration from Corner's design, aiming for a minimalist, interactive experience with cool scrolling effects and clean typography. It's a key reference point.


**Developing the Visual Concept**

My focus is now solidifying the visual direction. I'm leaning toward a "bento box" or card-style layout with smooth transitions, and using a bubble theme to create a bright, fresh, and interactive vibe. I've chosen colors, including a dark navy for text, to create good contrast. I'll need to work out a plan for content including a value proposition, installation guide, about the story, founder and contact info.


**Defining Design Elements**

Right now, I'm working to map the hex codes to a Tailwind config, planning clean sans-serif typography like Inter, and considering a grid layout, Bento style, with brand colors on varied backgrounds to establish a fresh, bright design. I'm focusing on an initial hero section, and interactive install guide, and a personal "story" section.


**Coding the Landing Page**

I'm now diving into the code itself, starting with setting up the project. I'm building out components like the hero section, bento-style feature grid, installation guide, about section, and footer. Right now, I'm working on the `components/Bubbles.tsx` file for a background animation using Framer Motion. I am also implementing large border-radius cards with sticky headers to emulate a Corner-style aesthetic. I've got this, and I'm feeling good.


**Outlining Implementation Strategy**

I'm now putting together the core implementation plan. I'm focusing on setting up the project structure first. I'll need to configure the Tailwind CDN and Google Fonts within `index.html`. Then, I'll mount the `App` component in `index.tsx`, and structure the main content within `App.tsx`. I'm also preparing standard `metadata.json` for general usage.


**Finalizing Component Architecture**

I'm now fully immersed in the component architecture. I am focusing on building out the visual appeal through the `Bubbles` component, and creating a strong hook with the `Hero` section. I'll maintain that "Corner" aesthetic in the `BentoGrid`, and providing practical information in the `InstallGuide`. I'm working to create an emotional connection with the `Story` component. I'm choosing the font 'Plus Jakarta Sans' for a modern feel. I'm focusing on the background bubbles with layers, using dark navy for accessible text. All the animations will be powered by framer-motion.


**Optimizing the Implementation**

Now I'm making final tweaks to the code. I'm focusing on the typography with "Plus Jakarta Sans". I'm also planning the bubble animation with randomized positions and sizes. The PWA installation guide is going to be a tabbed interface or side-by-side cards. I'm adding hover effects to the bento cards, and looking into a parallax effect for the bubbles. I'm making a final pass for imports, class names, and contrast ratios. I've also decided to use the placeholder "The Creator" for the founder.


**Adding Visual Details**

I'm now focusing on adding some final visual details. I'm going to generate the `dots.svg` icon to make the page more interactive. I'm also preparing for the next stage.

🌸 See something you love
You’re scrolling and find a profile, page, or website you don’t want to forget.

📋 Copy the link
No screenshots. No messy notes. Just copy the URL.

💖 Open Keepy
Create cute folders like Inspo, Dream Places, Creators, or Work Stuff.

🧷 Save & sort
Paste the link, choose a folder, and Keepy does the rest.

📝 Add little notes
Why you saved it? Ideas, reminders, vibes — it’s all up to you.

🔗 Tap & go
Open profiles straight from Keepy anytime you need them.

🤍 Share the love
Share your lists with friends — even if they don’t have Keepy. 4. the make it yours add keepy to your phone for ios and android  5. The Story Behind Keepy ✨

Hi, I’m Cassandra, the founder of Keepy 🤍

I take supporting local brands very seriously.
Social media has always been my favorite place to discover little gems —
cute cafés, boba spots, amazing artists, events, and fashion brands I instantly fall in love with.

But there was one problem…

Every time I wanted to go back and find something, I couldn’t.
The account name? Gone.
The website? Lost.
The screenshot? Buried somewhere between a thousand photos of my baby Elena 🍼💕

I tried everything — messy notes, endless saved posts, scrolling through DMs to friends like “I swear I sent this to you”… and it was honestly exhausting.

So I created Keepy ✨

An app where I could save the accounts and websites I love,
organize them into cute little folders,
add notes,
and actually find them again when I need them.

No more chaos.
No more screenshots.
Just everything I love — kept neatly in one place 🤍

The Story Behind Keepy ✨ {/* Decorative background vibe */}

Hi, I’m Cassandra, the founder of Keepy 🤍

I take supporting local brands very seriously. Social media has always been my favorite place to discover little gems — cute cafés, boba spots, amazing artists, events, and fashion brands I instantly fall in love with.

But there was one problem…

Every time I wanted to go back and find something, I couldn’t. The account name? Gone. The website? Lost. The screenshot? Buried somewhere between a thousand photos of my baby Elena 🍼💕

I tried everything — messy notes, endless saved posts, scrolling through DMs to friends like “I swear I sent this to you”… and it was honestly exhausting.

So I created Keepy ✨

An app where I could save the accounts and websites I love, organize them into cute little folders, add notes, and actually find them again when I need them.

No more chaos. No more screenshots. Just everything I love — kept neatly in one place 🤍

That’s how Keepy was born.

Cassandra
